summary: Complete academic thesis draft covering three chapters: Chapter 3 (Methodology) details the GIS techniques, data sources, classification procedures, terrain analysis, flood hazard mapping, and DE Africa shoreline extraction methods. Chapter 4 (Results) presents verified LULC statistics, transition matrices, slope instability encroachment, flood hazard findings, and shoreline change metrics for 187 transects. Chapter 5 (Discussion) interprets the urbanisation-geomorphic nexus, draws on literature, and synthesises the interconnection between urban expansion, terrain instability, and coastal erosion.
